data.title: Trump administration plans to restructure USAID and introduce blockchain technology to track aid distribution
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Trump administration is planning to reorganize the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) and integrate blockchain technology into its procurement system. The proposal suggests that USAID be renamed the "United States International Humanitarian Assistance Agency" and be directly under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of State.
According to a memorandum circulated within the State Department, all aid distribution will be securely tracked through blockchain to greatly improve security, transparency and traceability. At the same time, a payment model based on "results-oriented" will be established, rather than the traditional "input-oriented" to promote efficiency and innovation.
It is not clear whether the blockchain system will involve cryptocurrencies or stablecoins, or will only be used as a digital ledger. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Solana on-chain AI economic platform PioneerAI is about to go live
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News PioneerAI announced that it will officially launch on March 22, opening the PEA quota for a limited time claim (10 days), and launching token trading on the same day when the claim ends.

PioneerAI builds Solana on-chain aggregated AI ecosystem based on ChatGPT and Grok3 dual-core technology, and uses PEA tokens as the core to achieve decentralized circulation of computing power, data and services. It aims to build a global AI capability sharing network and promote the upgrade of blockchain to smart economic infrastructure.

data.title: Strategy increases Strife perpetual preferred stock offering size from $500 million to $722.5 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that Strategy founder Michael Saylor wrote that his Strategy company announced the completion of the pricing of Strife perpetual preferred shares (stock code: $STRF) and increased the size of this issuance from the originally planned US$500 million to US$722.5 million.

data.title: Binance Wallet will launch the "Zero Gas Fee Meme Weekend" event on March 21
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Binance Wallet announced that it will launch the "Zero Gas Fee Meme Weekend" event from 20:00 on March 21, 2025 to 20:00 on March 24 (Eastern Time 8). During this period, users will enjoy zero gas fees for exchange and cross-chain bridge transactions on the BNB Smart Chain through the Binance Wallet trading interface.
This event applies to all token transactions on the BNB chain, not just Meme coins. The daily limit for gas fee subsidies is 50 BNB, and the total amount of the event is 150 BNB, first come first served. Transactions initiated by connecting to third-party dApps are not covered by the subsidy.

data.title: Members of several U.S. states jointly wrote a letter calling on Elizabeth Warren to promote a legislative framework for crypto regulation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Former Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terrett posted on the X platform that legislators from multiple U.S. states recently jointly wrote to Senator Elizabeth Warren and the Democratic leadership, urging them to advance the legislative framework for cryptocurrency regulation to ensure that the industry develops under legal protection.

data.title: U.S. Lawmakers Urge Action on Cryptocurrency Regulation Framework
data.description: According to Odaily, former Fox Business journalist Eleanor Terrett shared on the X platform that lawmakers from multiple U.S. states have collectively written to Senator Elizabeth Warren and the Democratic leadership. They are urging the advancement of a legislative framework for cryptocurrency regulation to ensure the industry's development is safeguarded by law.
